config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once you give the collective too much power, it can easily be used to justify abuse and silence dissent. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first, because that is what keeps power accountable and prevents the majority from trampling the minority. Society matters, but it should be built through respecting people, not by treating individuals as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hasilnya sering melukai kebebasan sipil, minoritas, dan suara yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dengan batas yang jelas: hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama, bukan sesuatu yang bisa dikorbankan setiap kali ada klaim kepentingan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement as written: society should not take priority over the individual by default. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I have seen how “the public interest” can be used to pressure people into silence, conformity, or unfair treatment, especially when power is not accountable. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights mean ignoring the common good; public safety and social order matter, but they must have clear limits and cannot be an excuse to crush dissent or minorities. A good society is not one that sacrifices individuals easily, but one that protects them while still asking everyone to contribute responsibly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan daripada hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, terlalu sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kuasa negara yang akhirnya merugikan kelompok kecil dan suara yang tidak populer. Memang ada situasi darurat seperti wabah atau keamanan publik, tetapi pengecualian itu harus sempit, sementara hak individu tetap menjadi batas utama. Masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat bukan yang gampang mengorbankan orang, melainkan yang bisa melindungi kebebasan sambil tetap menjaga tanggung jawab bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement as a general rule: in Indonesia, I have seen how “for the sake of society” can become a convenient excuse for pressure, conformity, and abuse of power. Public order and shared responsibility do matter, but they should not be used to erase minority rights or silence people who are inconvenient to the majority. A society is stronger when it protects individuals firmly and only limits them in narrow, clearly justified cases. If the collective always comes first, the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru harus menjadi batas utama terhadap kekuasaan masyarakat atau negara. Pengalaman di Amerika menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau pembatasan yang akhirnya paling keras menimpa minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat memang nyata, tetapi hanya sah jika dibatasi ketat dan tidak menginjak kebebasan dasar. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keamanan bersama tanpa menyerahkan hak-hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
